reactionOptions.text: SP and BJP separately urge their cadres to respect the eventual verdict regardless of outcome, while reserving the right to pursue legal appeals.

risk.badOutcome: The reserved right to appeal is read by some as quietly preparing cadres to reject an unfavourable verdict.

reactionOptions.text: All major parties jointly issue a statement pledging to accept the court's verdict peacefully and pursue any further recourse only through legal channels.

risk.badOutcome: The fringe groups' refusal to join the pledge draws disproportionate media attention despite their small numbers.

reactionOptions.text: A handful of fringe groups decline to join the joint peace pledge, insisting they will respond as they see fit to the verdict.

newsItems.title: Allahabad High Court Delivers Landmark Ayodhya Verdict; State Stays Peaceful (2010-09)
newsItems.description: The Allahabad High Court rules on 30 September that the disputed 2.77-acre Ayodhya site be divided equally three ways among Ram Lalla, the Nirmohi Akhara and the Sunni Waqf Board, ending an eighteen-year wait since the Babri Masjid's demolition. Thanks to months of security preparation, Uttar Pradesh remains largely peaceful in the verdict's immediate aftermath, a outcome widely credited to the Mayawati government's law-and-order machinery.

reactionOptions.text: The government's extensive security preparations pay off as the state records no major violence, and Mayawati publicly credits the peaceful response to disciplined policing.

risk.badOutcome: Isolated minor scuffles are reported in a handful of districts overnight, slightly complicating the otherwise peaceful narrative.

reactionOptions.text: SP and BJP both welcome the peaceful outcome while stating they will study the verdict closely before deciding on further legal appeals.

risk.badOutcome: Early signals of dissatisfaction from both Hindu and Muslim litigants suggest the case is far from over at the Supreme Court.

reactionOptions.text: All major parties jointly commend the state machinery and community leaders for maintaining calm during a historically sensitive moment.

risk.badOutcome: A small fringe faction stages a brief, contained protest that draws outsized national media coverage.

reactionOptions.text: A handful of fringe groups on both sides express dissatisfaction with the verdict without immediately escalating to violence.

newsItems.title: Post-Verdict Calm Holds as Appeals Head to the Supreme Court (2010-10)
newsItems.description: In the weeks following the Ayodhya verdict, Uttar Pradesh remains calm as multiple parties to the case file appeals in the Supreme Court challenging the High Court's three-way division, with the apex court eventually staying the ruling pending further hearings. The state government, opposition and religious leaders alike cautiously welcome the sustained peace as validation of the preparation effort.

reactionOptions.text: The government maintains heightened security through the appeal period and credits sustained vigilance for the continued calm.

risk.badOutcome: The initiative runs into execution delays, drawing mild criticism.

reactionOptions.text: SP and BJP each welcome the calm while noting their respective allied litigants' plans to pursue the case vigorously at the Supreme Court.

risk.badOutcome: The attack is dismissed as partisan point-scoring, blunting its impact.

reactionOptions.text: Religious leaders and legislators across party lines jointly call for continued restraint regardless of how the Supreme Court eventually rules.

risk.badOutcome: Hardline factions on one side pull out of the consensus within days.

reactionOptions.text: A few fringe litigant-aligned groups decline to commit to continued restraint through the appeal process.

newsItems.title: SP and BJP Separately Sharpen Attacks Ahead of 2012 Assembly Cycle (2010-11)
newsItems.description: With just over a year until the next Assembly election, both SP and BJP separately intensify criticism of the Mayawati government's record on memorial spending, land acquisition and price rise, each seeking to consolidate their own distinct opposition space rather than cooperate against the government. The fragmented opposition landscape leaves BSP relatively unchallenged as a single bloc despite mounting individual criticisms.

reactionOptions.text: The government dismisses the sharpened attacks as premature election-year posturing from a divided opposition unable to unite against it.

risk.badOutcome: The initiative runs into execution delays, drawing mild criticism.

reactionOptions.text: SP and BJP separately launch district-level rallies previewing their 2012 campaign themes, each avoiding any hint of electoral cooperation with the other.

risk.badOutcome: The attack is dismissed as partisan point-scoring, blunting its impact.

reactionOptions.text: Political commentators across outlets call on the opposition to consider at least issue-based cooperation, even without a formal alliance, to hold the government accountable.

risk.badOutcome: Hardline factions on one side pull out of the consensus within days.

reactionOptions.text: Neither SP nor BJP responds to calls for even informal cooperation, each insisting on running independent campaigns.

newsItems.title: Year-End Reviews Highlight Rising Farmer Distress Over Unpaid Sugarcane Dues (2010-12)
newsItems.description: Year-end assessments of rural Uttar Pradesh highlight mounting farmer frustration in the western sugar belt over sugar mills' delayed payment of cane dues, even as the state government touts cumulative price increases achieved since 2007. Farmer unions warn that unresolved payment delays, layered atop the unresolved Yamuna Expressway land disputes, are building toward a larger reckoning in the region.

reactionOptions.text: The government orders mills with the largest pending dues to clear payments within a fixed deadline or face penalty action.

risk.badOutcome: Several mills miss the fixed deadline entirely, undercutting the government's enforcement credibility.

reactionOptions.text: SP and BJP-aligned farmer leaders demand a state-guaranteed dues-payment mechanism rather than reliance on mills' voluntary compliance.

risk.badOutcome: The demand for a state guarantee is dismissed by officials as fiscally unworkable given the state's existing budget constraints.

reactionOptions.text: Farmer unions across party lines jointly propose a escrow-based payment system to ensure timely disbursal regardless of individual mills' finances.

risk.badOutcome: The escrow proposal goes nowhere as mill owners resist any binding new payment structure.

reactionOptions.text: Several sugar mill owners decline to comment publicly on the scale of their pending dues.
